title: torilis infesta var. neglecta
intro: Torilis infesta var. neglecta, also known as Wild Chervil or Bee Flower, is a plant species in the carrot family that belongs to the genus Torilis. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which make it useful in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other nutrients that provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Torilis infesta var. neglecta is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its numerous health benefits.
